payload.answer: The proposed update appears overly atomized: 500 separate memory cards, one per CSV row. Consider repairing it by grouping related rows, extracting only durable facts, or keeping the data as a single structured table/reference instead of one card per row.
repair_options: Group related rows into fewer higher-level memory cards instead of one card per CSV row.
Keep the table as a single structured reference if the content is not durable personal memory.
Extract only stable, user-relevant facts and ignore incidental row-level details.
If row-level facts must be retained, ask the user which specific rows matter before creating any memory entries.
